symptoms: Trips out after a few seconds.

tests.error-log: BEATER OVERCURRENT trip on every start attempt since 6 am.
tests.inspect-beater: Beater spins freely by hand. No ice, no seizure, nothing jammed.
tests.continuity-test: Motor windings read perfect. The run capacitor reads dead — it's gone.

flavour: Saved a nine-hundred-dollar motor with a twenty-dollar can of electrons.
authenticityNote: A dead run capacitor makes a perfectly healthy single-phase motor hum, stall and trip — the textbook test-before-you-swap fault (GDD §2.2). Condemning the motor is the classic expensive wrong answer.
